title: jurinea helenae
intro: Jurinea helenae, also known as Helen's knapweed, is a plant species within the family Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe, due to its medicinal properties. The plant contains sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and protecting against cell damage. Jurinea helenae can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion for consumption, or used in tincture or capsule form as a dietary supplement.
